pi_items.source: Levin Center for Oversight and Democracy, American Archive of Public Broadcasting, and Senate.gov confirm the first phase is titled 'Watergate Investigations' not 'Phase I: Watergate Investigation'

pi_items.metadata_value: Select Committee on Presidential Campaign Activities
pi_items.verified_value: Select Committee on Presidential Campaign Activities
pi_items.source: U.S. Senate official website (senate.gov), Wikipedia, and Levin Center all confirm this is the official name of the Senate committee established via S.Res. 60 in 1973
Multiple sources including U.S. Senate official website confirm the committee was a Senate select committee, not a House committee
Congress.gov and Wikipedia confirm the 93rd Congress 1st Session ran from January 3, 1973 to December 22, 1973, which encompasses May 17, 1973
HISTORY.com, PBS News, Levin Center, American Archive of Public Broadcasting, and U.S. Senate sources all confirm May 17, 1973 as the date the Watergate hearings opened

pi_items.verified_value: Washington, D.C. (Russell Senate Office Building, Senate Caucus Room)
pi_items.source: Web search results specifically mention the hearings took place in the Senate Caucus Room of the Russell Senate Office Building in Washington, D.C.

pi_items.verified_value: Hearings before the Select Committee on Presidential Campaign Activities
pi_items.source: Multiple sources reference the full official title in hearing documents as 'Hearings before the Select Committee on Presidential Campaign Activities' - the metadata snippet is a truncated but accurate opening phrase

privileged_info_summary: The Senate Watergate hearings occurred on May 17, 1973, in Washington, D.C., before the Select Committee on Presidential Campaign Activities during the 93rd Congress, 1st Session. The first phase of hearings is officially titled 'Watergate Investigations' (not 'Phase I: Watergate Investigation' as the metadata claims). The hearings were conducted at the Russell Senate Office Building Senate Caucus Room and were nationally televised, lasting 51 days with 237 hours of witness testimony. These are historically documented real events with extensive archival records.
